settings.brand.description: Centralize your organization's logo, icon, colours and font. New apps and chatbots inherit this brand, and surfaces that haven't overridden a value follow it live.

settings.brand.colors_hint: The accent colour drives buttons, links and highlights across your surfaces.

system.mcp.page_description: Connect Claude Code and Claude web to this organization over MCP.

apps.access.description: Choose who in your organization can use this app and in which role. Admins (you, the app owner, and organization owners) always have full access.
apps.access.mode_open: Open — every member gets the default role; assign to elevate.
apps.access.mode_allowlist: Allowlist — only assigned members can use the app.
apps.access.no_roles: This app has no roles yet. Add roles in the manifest to assign them.
apps.access.no_members: Your organization has no other members to assign yet.
apps.builder.resize_panels: Drag to resize panels (double-click to reset)
apps.builder.workflows.empty_state: No workflows yet. Create one or ask Claude to build it.
apps.builder.workflows.run_only_manual: Only manual-trigger workflows can be run from here.
apps.builder.workflows.verify_tooltip: Dry-run the workflow with writes simulated, and check it behaves.
apps.builder.workflows.delete_confirm: Delete this workflow? Direct undo from the trash isn't supported yet.
apps.builder.workflows.approval.heading: Approval needed
apps.builder.workflows.panel.date_field_: This object has no date or datetime fields.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.channel_non: No chat channels yet — create a WhatsApp or widget channel first.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.integration: No integrations yet — create one (and set its webhook signing secret) first.
Set the provider's webhook signing secret on the integration, then point the provider at its webhook URL.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.poll_action: No connected actions yet — create a REST/GraphQL tool bound to an integration first.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.items_path_: Dot-path to the array in the response. Leave blank if the response is itself the array.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.watermark_p: A field on each item that only grows (e.g. id or created_at) — used to detect items not seen before.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.email_hint: Configure the email provider + signing secret on the integration, then point the provider at its email webhook URL.
apps.builder.workflows.cron_preset.month: On the 1st of each month at 9:00 AM
apps.builder.workflows.steps.log.label: Log
apps.builder.workflows.steps.log.descrip: Print a message to the workflow log.
apps.builder.workflows.steps.set_variabl: Store a value in vars for later steps to reference.
apps.builder.workflows.steps.record_crea: Create a new record in an object.
apps.builder.workflows.steps.record_upda: Update fields on an existing record.
apps.builder.workflows.steps.record_quer: Read records with filters, sort and limit.
apps.builder.workflows.steps.branch.desc: Branch the flow on a condition. Editing the branches is via chat for now.
apps.builder.workflows.steps.ai_complete: Call an AI model with a prompt and store the response.
apps.builder.workflows.steps.http_reques: Make an HTTP request to an external URL.
apps.builder.workflows.steps.connector_c: Call a typed action on a connected integration.
apps.builder.workflows.connector.gated_h: A write to an external system — it runs behind an approval gate.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.connector_n: No integrations connected yet. Ask the builder to connect one.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.connector_u: This integration isn't authorized yet. Authorize it before the flow can run.
This action has no declared inputs — map values in the advanced JSON below.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.filter_empt: No filter — fires on every record.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.filter_adva: This filter uses advanced logic (groups or NOT). Edit it via chat or JSON, or clear it to rebuild here.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.webhook_sec: Sign the request body with HMAC-SHA256 and send it as the X-Sapiensly-Signature header.
apps.builder.workflows.panel.output_vari: Other steps can reference the result like this:
apps.builder.workflows.panel.advanced_hi: For fields without a dedicated input (values, filter, headers, etc).
apps.builder.visual_review_tooltip: Send a screenshot of this page to the AI for a visual review.
apps.builder.autonomous_hint: Autonomous mode: work the build plan step by step on its own until it's done.
apps.builder.new_conversation_confirm: Start a new conversation? The current one will be archived but the manifest changes you already approved stay.
apps.builder.empty_prompt: Tell the AI what to add, change or remove. Example: "Add a 'Clientes' object with a name and email field, then a page listing them."
apps.builder.revert_confirm: Undo this change? The manifest will roll back to the state before this turn.
apps.builder.plan.heading: Plan
apps.builder.plan.discard_message: Discard that plan, let's not build it.
apps.builder.provision.heading: New connection
apps.builder.provision.title: This flow needs {name}
apps.builder.provision.credentials_note: You'll authorize in {name}'s own window. Sapiensly never sees your password.
apps.builder.show_full_summary: Click to see the full summary
apps.builder.attachment_invalid_type: Only PNG, JPG, WebP or GIF images are accepted.
apps.builder.attachment_too_large: The image is too large (5 MB max).
apps.builder.attachment_default_message: Take a look at the attached image.
apps.builder.drop_to_attach: Drop the image to attach it
apps.builder.slash.seed.description: Generate realistic demo records for an object.
apps.builder.slash.seed.prompt: Generate {count} realistic demo records for the object whose name or slug is '{object}' using the `seed_records` tool. Before calling it, read the manifest to confirm the available fields and their types (including single_select options — use option SLUGS, not display names). If the object has relations, use `inspect_records` to obtain real ids of the related objects. If no object matches '{object}', ask me which one I mean before creating anything. Data must make sense for my app's domain (realistic names, dates, amounts, descriptions — no 'lorem ipsum'). At the end, tell me how many records you created and summarize any errors.
apps.builder.slash.clean.description: Detect orphan fields, blocks, or references and propose removing them.
apps.builder.slash.clean.prompt: Review the full manifest and tell me whether there are orphan blocks, fields or references (to IDs that no longer exist, fields not used in any page, workflows pointing at deleted objects, etc). For each finding, propose removal via propose_change and summarize at the end what was removed.
apps.builder.slash.duplicate_page.descri: Duplicate a page and all of its blocks with fresh IDs.
apps.builder.slash.duplicate_page.prompt: Duplicate the page whose name or slug is '{page}'. Generate new IDs for the page and for every nested block (including blocks inside tabs / accordion / split_view / card_grid). The new page's name must end with ' (copy)' and its slug must be unique. Don't touch the original page. Apply the change with propose_change.
apps.builder.slash.explain.description: Explain in plain language what the current page does.
apps.builder.slash.explain.prompt: Explain in plain language, in 4-6 bullets, what the page '{currentPage}' does in this app: what data it shows, what blocks it contains, what actions the user can perform and what workflows fire from here. Don't propose changes — just describe.
apps.builder.slash.translate.description: Translate every user-facing label in the manifest into the given language.
apps.builder.slash.translate.prompt: Translate ALL user-facing text in the manifest to '{language}': page names, block titles, column labels, field names, descriptions, submit/cancel labels, modal titles, etc. DO NOT touch IDs, slugs, expressions, action types or any technical identifier. Apply all changes in a single propose_change.
apps.builder.wireframe.tooltip: Reconstruct an existing UI (screenshot, Figma / claude.ai URL, or HTML) as a manifest for your business.
apps.builder.wireframe.title: Import from wireframe
apps.builder.wireframe.description: We show Claude what you attach so it can scaffold objects, pages and blocks that recreate that design in your app.
apps.builder.wireframe.image_hint: Upload a screenshot of the wireframe (PNG, JPG, WebP or GIF, 5 MB max).
apps.builder.wireframe.url_hint: Paste a Figma link, claude.ai/share URL, a regular web page or a public screenshot. We try to pull the preview image and visible text.
apps.builder.wireframe.html_hint: Paste the wireframe's HTML (View source / Inspect element). Works for claude.ai components or any static UI.
apps.builder.wireframe.business_placehol: e.g. I sell plants to offices in Mexico City and track customers and deliveries.
apps.builder.wireframe.business_hint: Helps Claude adapt field names and logic to your domain.
apps.builder.wireframe.no_source: Attach an image, paste a URL, or paste HTML before continuing.
apps.builder.chip.app_from_scratch.label: Create app from scratch
apps.builder.chip.app_from_scratch.promp: I want to build an app from scratch. Ask me about my business and propose the initial objects, pages and workflows.
apps.builder.chip.first_object.label: Add my first object
apps.builder.chip.first_object.prompt: Add the first object to my app. Ask me what I want to model and propose its fields.
apps.builder.chip.suggest_objects.label: Suggest objects for my business
apps.builder.chip.suggest_objects.prompt: Ask me what my business is about and suggest a set of objects with their fields and relations that would make sense to model.
apps.builder.chip.add_relation.label: Add a relation
apps.builder.chip.add_relation.prompt: I want to add a relation between two objects. Ask me which two and what cardinality.
apps.builder.chip.add_validations.label: Add validations
apps.builder.chip.add_validations.prompt: Review every object's fields and suggest reasonable validations (required, min/max, format) where they make sense. Wait for my confirmation before applying.
apps.builder.chip.add_field.label: Add a field
apps.builder.chip.add_field.prompt: I want to add a field to one of my objects. Ask me which object and what type.
apps.builder.chip.seed_data.label: Generate sample data
apps.builder.chip.seed_data.prompt: Generate 20 realistic demo records for each of my objects, respecting existing relations.
apps.builder.chip.empty_page.add_table.p: Add a table block listing records of this page's main object. Pick the most relevant columns.
apps.builder.chip.empty_page.add_dashboa: Build a starter dashboard for this page: a row of stats (3-4 KPIs), a table with recent records, and a trend chart.
apps.builder.chip.empty_page.add_form.pr: Add a form block in create mode for this page's main object, with all the relevant fields.
apps.builder.chip.add_chart.label: Add a chart
apps.builder.chip.add_chart.prompt: Add a chart to this page. Pick a metric that makes sense to visualize and the best chart type.
apps.builder.chip.add_action_column.prom: Add action columns (Edit / Delete) to this page's main table.
apps.builder.chip.improve_layout.label: Improve the layout
apps.builder.chip.improve_layout.prompt: Review this page and propose improvements to layout, visual hierarchy and spacing. Wait for my confirmation before applying.
apps.builder.chip.add_workflow.label: Add a workflow
apps.builder.chip.add_workflow.prompt: Add a useful workflow for this app. Ask me what event should fire it and what steps to run.
apps.builder.chip.explain_manifest.label: Explain this manifest
apps.builder.chip.explain_manifest.promp: Explain in plain language, in 4-6 bullets, what this app does: which objects, pages and workflows it has.
apps.builder.chip.find_orphans.label: Find orphans
apps.builder.chip.find_orphans.prompt: Review the manifest and tell me whether there are orphan blocks, fields or references to non-existent IDs. Propose removing what's left over.
apps.builder.chip.optimize.label: Suggest optimizations
apps.builder.chip.optimize.prompt: Review the manifest and suggest optimizations: duplicate fields, blocks that could be merged, missing validations, inconsistent naming.

documents.create.ai.prompt_placeholder_m: e.g. A markdown onboarding guide for new hires, covering day-one setup, point-of-contact table, and a 30-60-90 plan.

tools.config.function.name: Function Name
tools.config.function.name_description: The name that will be used when calling this function
tools.config.function.description_exampl: Gets the current weather for a given location
tools.config.function.description_hint: Description of what this function does
tools.config.function.guidance: Define a function the agent can call. The name and description tell the model when to use it; the parameters are the inputs it must provide.
tools.config.function.no_params: No parameters yet. Add one, or switch to JSON for a nested schema.
tools.config.function.json_hint: Raw JSON Schema. Use this for nested objects or array items the builder can't express.
tools.config.rest.guidance: Call a REST endpoint. Pick an integration for the base URL and auth, then define the path and parameters the agent fills in.
tools.config.graphql.guidance: Run a GraphQL query or mutation. Pick an integration for the endpoint and auth, then write the operation and its variables.
tools.config.mcp.guidance: Connect to an MCP server. The agent gains every tool the server exposes — no per-tool wiring needed.
tools.config.database.guidance: Run a parameterized SQL query. Keep read-only on unless the agent truly needs to write.
tools.config.group.guidance: Bundle several tools so they can be assigned to an agent together.
tools.config.mcp.endpoint_description: The URL of the MCP server
tools.config.mcp.auth_description: How to authenticate with the MCP server
tools.config.mcp.auth_credentials_note: Authentication credentials will be configured securely after creating the tool.
tools.config.mcp.oauth_integration_descr: The MCP server uses your personal access token for this integration. Authorization is per-user — each member authorizes once and tokens refresh automatically.
tools.config.mcp.oauth_save_first: Save the tool, then authorize from its page.
tools.config.mcp.oauth_no_integrations: You don't have any OAuth 2.0 integrations yet. Create one to authenticate this MCP server with web auth.
tools.config.mcp.no_connections: No MCP integrations yet. Add an MCP server in Integrations first.
tools.config.connection.label: Integration
tools.config.connection.hint: Borrow the base URL and authentication from an integration instead of configuring them here.
tools.config.connection.inherits: Base URL and authentication come from the selected integration.
tools.config.connection.none: No integrations yet — connect a system first.
tools.config.database.name: Database Name
tools.config.database.read_only_hint: When enabled, only SELECT queries are allowed. Disable to allow INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE operations.
tools.config.connection.db_hint: Borrow the driver, host and credentials from an integration instead of configuring them here.
tools.config.connection.db_inherits: The driver, host and credentials come from the selected integration.
tools.config.group.no_tools: No active function or MCP tools available. Create and activate tools first to add them to a group.

system.ai_providers.description: Configure AI providers and API keys for your account
system.ai_providers.no_providers_descrip: Add an AI provider to start creating agents with LLM capabilities.
system.cloud_providers.title: Cloud Providers
system.cloud_providers.description: Configure object storage and database connections for this workspace
system.cloud_providers.storage_descripti: Store documents and media in your own S3-compatible bucket. Leave empty to use the platform default.
system.cloud_providers.database_descript: Point your custom tables and vector store to your own PostgreSQL instance.
system.cloud_providers.using_global_stor: No workspace storage configured. The platform has no default either — ask your platform administrator.
system.cloud_providers.using_global_data: No workspace database configured. The platform has no default either — ask your platform administrator.
system.cloud_providers.override_active_s: Your workspace overrides the platform default
system.cloud_providers.override_active_d: Your workspace overrides the platform default
system.cloud_providers.remove_override_c: This will delete the workspace configuration and fall back to the platform default. Continue?
system.cloud_providers.read_only_notice: Only organization owners can configure cloud providers.
system.cloud_providers.vector.heading: Vector store
system.cloud_providers.vector.descriptio: The pgvector extension and chunks table are required to store embeddings on your database.
system.cloud_providers.vector.unreachabl: Could not reach the database — verify credentials and network access.
system.cloud_providers.vector.schema_mis: Vector store schema not bootstrapped yet
system.cloud_providers.vector.manual_ins: Ask your database administrator to run: CREATE EXTENSION vector;
system.integrations.title: Integrations
system.integrations.description: Connect third-party services and external tools to your workspace
system.integrations.empty_description: Connect your first external API to save reusable requests and test them in a single click.
system.integrations.starter.title: Connect your first system
system.integrations.starter.description: Pick how you want to start. A connection holds the base URL and credentials your tools and agents reuse.
system.integrations.starter.rest_desc: Connect any HTTP API with a base URL and auth.
system.integrations.starter.mcp_desc: Connect an MCP server and expose its tools to agents.
system.integrations.starter.database_des: Connect an external database for agents to query.
system.integrations.oauth2_discover.hint: Paste the MCP server (or issuer) URL. We'll discover the OAuth endpoints and register a client automatically.
system.integrations.oauth2_discover.succ: Discovered and registered a client automatically. Just save and authorize.
Discovered the OAuth endpoints. This server doesn't support automatic registration — enter your Client ID and Client Secret below.
system.integrations.oauth2_discover.fail: Could not auto-configure from this URL. Enter the endpoints manually below.
system.integrations.delete_confirm: Delete this integration and all its requests?
system.integrations.templates.title: Pre-made connections
system.integrations.templates.descriptio: Start from a preconfigured integration — paste your credential and save.
system.integrations.oauth2.authorize_hin: Click to open the provider in a new tab, sign in, and grant the requested scopes. You'll be redirected back here with the connection active.
system.integrations.oauth2.connected_hin: This integration holds a valid access token. Re-authorize if the provider has revoked the grant or the scopes have changed.
system.integrations.oauth2.incomplete_hi: The OAuth endpoints and Client ID must be filled before members can authorize. Open the form to complete them.
system.integrations.oauth2.client_ready_: Authorization is per-user and happens from your MCP tools. Link this integration in a tool, then authorize it there.
system.integrations.form.mcp_description: Connect an MCP server and expose its tools to your agents.
system.integrations.form.mcp_discover_hi: Paste the server URL. Optionally, Discover auto-configures its endpoints and registers a client for you.
system.integrations.form.mcp_basics_hint: Name it and point it at the server.
system.integrations.form.db_description: Connect an external database your agents can query to analyze and transform its data.
system.integrations.form.db_connection_h: How to reach the database. Credentials are encrypted at rest.
system.integrations.form.ssh_tunnel_hint: For databases only reachable through a bastion / jump host.
system.integrations.form.ssh_passphrase_: Only if the private key is encrypted.
system.integrations.form.ssh_key_hint: The bastion's host key is trusted on first use.
system.integrations.mcp_auth.label: Access
system.integrations.mcp_auth.public_hint: No sign-in — anyone in your org can use it.
system.integrations.mcp_auth.oauth_hint: Each user signs in through the provider.
system.integrations.mcp_auth.per_user_no: Members authorize individually from the tool that uses this connection.
system.integrations.mcp_auth.token_hint: A static token sent on every request.
system.integrations.mcp_auth.oauth_confi: OAuth client configured — nothing else to fill in.
system.integrations.mcp_auth.oauth_disco: Run Discover on the Server URL above, or open advanced settings to configure the client manually.
system.integrations.form.basics_hint: Give this integration a clear name and a base URL you'll append paths to.
system.integrations.form.name: Name
system.integrations.form.authentication_: How this integration identifies itself against the external API.
system.integrations.form.default_headers: Included on every request under this integration unless overridden.
system.integrations.form.visibility_hint: Controls who in your workspace can see this integration.
system.integrations.form.allow_insecure_: Only sysadmins should enable this. Use for self-signed staging endpoints.
system.integrations.form.is_mcp_hint: MCP servers expose tools over the protocol. This hides the REST request builder and is consumed by linking the integration in an MCP tool.
system.integrations.mcp.panel_hint: This is an MCP server, not a REST API — there are no requests to build here. Link it in an MCP tool to use it, and authorize it per-user from there.
system.integrations.db.panel_hint: Expose specific queries against this database as tools your agents can run.
system.integrations.auth.api_key.name: Key name
system.integrations.auth.kept_secret: Leave blank to keep the saved value
system.integrations.webhook.title: Inbound webhook
system.integrations.webhook.hint: Let this provider trigger app workflows (the integration.event trigger).
system.integrations.webhook.secret_hint: The provider's webhook signing secret. We verify each delivery's HMAC-SHA256 signature against it.
system.integrations.webhook.url_event_hi: For the integration.event trigger. Sign the request body with the secret below.
system.integrations.auth.none_note: No authentication — requests are sent as-is. Use this for public endpoints.
system.integrations.auth.purpose.api_key: A key sent in a header or query param.
system.integrations.auth.purpose.bearer: A static token sent as a Bearer header.
system.integrations.auth.purpose.custom_: One or more fixed headers you define.
system.integrations.auth.purpose.oauth2_: Each user signs in through the provider.
system.integrations.auth.explainer.api_k: Enter the key name and value. Choose whether it rides in a header or the query string.
system.integrations.auth.explainer.beare: Paste the token. It's sent as Authorization: Bearer on every request.
system.integrations.auth.explainer.basic: Enter the username and password. They're sent as an HTTP Basic header.
system.integrations.auth.explainer.custo: Add the exact headers this service expects on every request.
system.integrations.auth.explainer.oauth: Paste the token URL and client credentials. We fetch and refresh tokens automatically.
Paste your OAuth app's client ID and secret. Each user authorizes from the tool that uses this connection.
system.integrations.auth.oauth2.redirect: Register this exact URL as the callback in your provider's OAuth app.
system.integrations.show.actions_hint: Tools your agents can run through this connection.
system.integrations.show.no_actions: No tools use this connection yet — create one to let agents act through it.
system.integrations.show.no_requests: No requests yet — add one to start
system.integrations.show.no_environments: No environments yet — add one to scope variables per stage (dev / staging / prod).
system.integrations.show.requests_hint: Saved requests you can reuse from agents, flows, and tests.
system.integrations.show.environments_hi: Stage-scoped variables (dev / staging / prod) that requests interpolate at runtime.
system.integrations.show.edit_config_hin: Base URL, authentication and default headers.
system.integrations.show.edit_config_bod: Open the form to change endpoints, swap credentials or toggle advanced flags.
system.integrations.show.danger_hint: Irreversible actions that affect everyone with access to this integration.
admin.global_ai.title: Global AI
admin.global_ai.description: Manage the platform-wide AI catalog and default providers

wipe_dialog.title: This will delete knowledge data
wipe_dialog.description_tenant: Changing the database provider will permanently delete all knowledge bases, documents, and embeddings in this workspace. This cannot be undone.
wipe_dialog.description_global: Changing the global database provider will permanently delete all knowledge bases, documents, and embeddings in every workspace that has not configured its own database override. This cannot be undone.
system.ai_provider_form.add_description: Connect an AI provider and choose the models it exposes.
system.ai_provider_form.add_provider_des: Select the provider and enter its API credentials.
system.ai_provider_form.models_descripti: Pick the chat and/or embeddings model this provider should offer.

system.ai_provider_form.edit_description: Update provider configuration and available models

system.ai_provider_form.same_as_llm: Same provider as LLM — reuse credentials

botFlows.panel.title: Node Properties
botFlows.panel.match_file_hint: Routes on the file the visitor uploaded. Use a rule pattern like image, pdf, or document.
botFlows.panel.agents_team_description: Configure the 3-layer Multi-Agent that will handle this step.

botFlows.panel.layer_disabled_hint: Enable this layer to configure its agent.

botFlows.panel.agent_none_for_role: No agents of this role yet — create one first.
botFlows.palette.agent_description: An AI brain in the bot
botFlows.assistant.title: Describe your bot
botFlows.assistant.hint: Describe what your bot should do and we'll generate a starting flow you can refine.
botFlows.assistant.placeholder: e.g. A support bot that greets visitors, answers FAQs from our docs, and escalates refunds to an action agent.
botFlows.assistant.followup_placeholder: Ask for a change… e.g. add a menu option for billing
botFlows.assistant.error: Couldn't update the flow. Try again.
botFlows.modal.create_description: This agent will be created in your account and selected for this layer.

admin.users.invite.description: They'll receive an email with a verification link to finish setup.
admin.users.invite.name: Name (optional)

admin.users.delete.plan_transfer: Their resources will transfer to the owner of {org}.
admin.users.delete.plan_cascade: Their resources will be deleted alongside the user.
admin.access.heading: Access
admin.access.description: Registration, verification, 2FA, and allowlists — tighten or open at will.
admin.access.registration.title: Registration
admin.access.registration.description: Who can sign up and whether they must verify first.
admin.access.registration.open_descripti: When off, only invited users can sign in.
admin.access.registration.verify_descrip: New accounts must click the verification link before signing in.
admin.access.auth.title: Authentication
admin.access.auth.two_factor_description: Users without 2FA will be forced through setup on next sign-in.
admin.access.auth.two_factor_warning_bod: {count} users don't have 2FA configured. They'll be forced through setup on their next sign-in.
admin.access.auth.two_factor_warning_emp: Every user already has 2FA — safe to enable.
admin.access.domains.title: Domain allowlist
admin.access.domains.description: Restrict invites to specific email domains. Leave empty to allow any domain.
admin.access.domains.open: Empty list — any domain is accepted.
admin.access.domains.gated: Invites must belong to one of these domains.
admin.access.ips.title: IP allowlist
admin.access.ips.description: Restrict admin access to specific ranges. Optional hardening.
admin.access.ips.enable_description: Off by default — turn on once you have your office / VPN ranges.
admin.access.posture.title: Security posture
admin.access.posture.description: Quick look at where hardening is still pending.
admin.ai.heading: Global AI
admin.ai.defaults.description: Per-module primary + fallback model. The fallback is used if the primary errors or is rate-limited.
admin.ai.defaults.fallback_hint: The fallback is tried automatically when the primary errors.
admin.ai.defaults.module.chat.title: Chat
admin.ai.defaults.module.summary_short.d: Cheap, fast model for short automatic summaries — e.g. naming a chat from its first message.
admin.ai.defaults.module.summary_large.d: Model that condenses long, costly conversations into an optimized summarized context.
admin.ai.defaults.module.builder.title: App Builder
admin.ai.defaults.module.builder.descrip: Model that powers the app builder agent.
admin.ai.defaults.module.flows.title: Flows
admin.ai.defaults.module.flows.descripti: Model for flow steps not pinned to an agent.
admin.ai.defaults.module.chatbots.title: Chatbots
admin.ai.defaults.module.chatbots.descri: Model for chatbot/widget replies when the agent sets none.
admin.ai.catalog.description: Which models are exposed platform-wide — toggle per driver.
admin.ai.usage.description: Call volume and cost, by day and driver — aggregated across every tenant.
admin.ai.defaults.chat_description: Primary and fallback models used whenever an agent produces a reply.
admin.ai.defaults.streaming_description: Tokens arrive at the client as they're generated — off forces a single block response.
admin.ai.defaults.embedding_description: The model used to vectorise Knowledge Base content.
admin.ai.defaults.embedding_hint: Changing this triggers a full reindex of every Knowledge Base.
admin.ai.defaults.embedding_swap_body: Every Knowledge Base will be re-vectorised with the new model. Active retrievals fall back to the previous model until the rebuild finishes.
admin.ai.defaults.embedding_swap_note: Reindex runs in the background — no click-to-finish required.
admin.ai.defaults.sampling_description: Defaults applied when a caller doesn't override them.
admin.ai.providers.description: Register the API keys for the AI providers — applied across every tenant unless overridden.
admin.ai.providers.direct_description: First-party providers. Add an API key to make their models reachable platform-wide.
admin.ai.providers.broker_description: Aggregators that route to many upstream models through a single key.
admin.ai.providers.add_description: Paste the key from the provider's dashboard. It applies to every tenant unless overridden.
admin.ai.providers.rotate_description: Paste the new key. The old key stops working the moment you save.
admin.ai.providers.models_description: Pick which OpenRouter models to expose in the catalog and model pickers.
admin.ai.providers.models_load_failed: Couldn't load the OpenRouter model list.
admin.ai.catalog.intro: Which models appear in the Defaults dropdowns and agent pickers.
admin.ai.catalog.test_hint: Run a minimal real invocation to verify this model id works.
admin.ai.catalog.provider_not_connected_: Connect this model's provider with an API key in the Providers tab before enabling it.
admin.ai.usage.empty_body: Call volume, cost, and per-driver breakdown land once the instrumentation pipeline is wired.
admin.cloud.heading: Global Cloud
admin.cloud.description: Storage, database, and vector search — what powers the stack underneath.
admin.cloud.storage.title: Object storage
admin.cloud.storage.capacity_hint: Usage is fetched by a nightly roll-up job — it lands here when the job ships.
admin.cloud.storage.empty: No global storage provider configured yet.
admin.cloud.database.title: Primary database
admin.cloud.database.description: Relational store for users, settings, and conversations.
admin.cloud.database.empty: Database is unreachable or not configured.
admin.cloud.pgvector.title: pgvector
admin.cloud.pgvector.description: Vector search — required for Knowledge Base retrieval.
admin.cloud.pgvector.col.name: Name
admin.cloud.pgvector.no_indexes: No vector indexes yet — none will exist until the first KB is chunked.
admin.cloud.pgvector.disabled: pgvector is not installed on this database. Run CREATE EXTENSION vector on the host to enable retrieval.
admin.cloud.tenancy.title: Multi-tenancy
admin.cloud.tenancy.description: Database-level isolation — a platform/tenant schema split enforced by Postgres roles and Row-Level Security.
admin.cloud.tenancy.role.platform: Platform — control-plane runtime, no tenant access
admin.cloud.tenancy.rls_complete: All tenant tables are protected by Row-Level Security.
admin.cloud.tenancy.rls_gap: {gap} tenant table(s) are not yet protected.
admin.cloud.tenancy.empty: Schema-split introspection is only available on PostgreSQL.
admin.cloud.redis.title: Redis
admin.cloud.redis.description: In-memory layer backing cache, queues, and sessions.
admin.cloud.redis.empty: Redis is unreachable. Check REDIS_HOST / REDIS_PORT and that the server is running.
admin.stack.heading: Stack
admin.stack.description: Every piece of software this platform is built on — versions and status at a glance.
slides.index.title: Slides
slides.index.description: Presentations created from your conversations and data — open one to present full-screen.
slides.builder.title: Slide Builder
slides.builder.save_failed: The change could not be saved.
slides.builder.send_failed: The message could not be sent — try again.
slides.builder.chat_placeholder: Tell the assistant what to change…
slides.builder.chat_empty: Ask for anything: "make slide 3 punchier", "add a chart with tasks by status from my Growth Tracker", "tighten the whole narrative". Changes apply to the preview live.
slides.builder.live_value: Live value — recalculated from app data on every open.
slides.builder.live_chart: Live chart — labels and series are recalculated from app data on every open; the fields below are the fallback.
slides.builder.field.title: Title
slides.builder.field.subtitle: Subtitle
slides.builder.field.heading: Heading
slides.builder.field.label: Label
slides.layout.title: Title
slides.index.empty_description: Ask the assistant in Chat to turn a strategy, plan or results review into a presentation, and it will appear here.
app_v2.dashboard.heading: Dashboard
app_v2.dashboard.description: Welcome back. Your agents, flows, and integrations at a glance.
app_v2.dashboard.welcome_body: Build an agent, wire it to a knowledge base, and connect it to a channel — the three moves that take you from idea to live automation.
app_v2.flows.heading: Flows
app_v2.flows.description: Deterministic conversation graphs — handoffs, tool calls, and branching logic for your agents.
app_v2.chatbots.heading: Chatbots
app_v2.chatbots.description: Customer-facing bots with knowledge bases, tools, and streaming responses.
app_v2.agents.heading: Agents
app_v2.agents.description: Standalone agents that execute tools and reason with your private data.
app_v2.documents.heading: Documents
app_v2.documents.description: Source material for your agents — uploaded, parsed, and ready to be chunked into a Knowledge Base.
app_v2.integrations.heading: Integrations
app_v2.integrations.description: Connections to external systems — base URL, credentials, and environments your tools reuse.
app_v2.common.empty_body: Click the button above to create the first one.
chat.delete_project_confirm: Delete this project? Its chats are kept (just ungrouped).
chat.project_dialog_hint: Group related chats and give the assistant standing instructions for them.
chat.project_knowledge_hint: Chats in this project search these knowledge bases for relevant context.
chat.no_knowledge_bases: You have no knowledge bases yet. Create one under Knowledge Bases.
chat.project_instructions_placeholder: Custom instructions (optional) — e.g. "Always answer in formal English and cite sources."
chat.delete_confirm: Delete this chat? This cannot be undone.
chat.tools.label: Tools
chat.disclaimer: AI can make mistakes. Double-check important info.
chat.model_picker.label: Model
chat.agent_picker.label: Agents
chat.question.label: Question
chat.send_failed: Couldn't send the message. Try again.
chat.empty.subtitle: Pick a model, attach files, and start chatting.
debate.delete_confirm: Delete this debate? This cannot be undone.
debate.round.label: Round {number} · {type}
debate.moderator.title: Moderator
debate.conclusions.title: Conclusions
debate.setup.heading: AI Debate
debate.setup.subheading: State a problem and let several models deliberate until they reach consensus.
debate.setup.topic_placeholder: What should the council deliberate? e.g. Should we build or buy our CRM?
debate.setup.cta_hint_min: Select at least 2 participants to begin.
debate.setup.cta_hint_ready: The council will deliberate and converge on an answer.
debate.setup.no_models: No models available. Configure an AI provider first.
debate.setup.moderator_hint: Judges consensus and writes the conclusions.
debate.setup.rounds_hint: Stops early once the council converges.
debate.setup.suggestion_1: Should we build or buy our CRM?
debate.setup.suggestion_2: Is a 4-day work week good for our company?
debate.setup.suggestion_3: Which pricing model should we adopt: subscription or usage-based?
apps.builder.rate_limited: Too many requests — wait {seconds}s and try again.
admin.ai.defaults.no_models: no models — enable some in Catalog
admin.ai.defaults.module.embeddings.desc: Model that vectorises Knowledge Base content for retrieval.
admin.ai.defaults.module.coding.title: Coding
admin.ai.defaults.module.coding.descript: Model agents hand off code-heavy tasks to.
admin.ai.defaults.module.ocr_pdf.title: OCR — PDF
admin.ai.defaults.module.ocr_pdf.descrip: Vision model used to extract text from PDF documents.
admin.ai.defaults.module.image_generatio: Model used to generate images from a prompt.
admin.ai.defaults.module.audio_recogniti: Speech-to-text model used to transcribe audio.
admin.ai.defaults.module.speech_generati: Text-to-speech model used to synthesize audio.
admin.ai.defaults.module.reranking.title: Reranking
admin.ai.defaults.module.reranking.descr: Model used to reorder retrieval results by relevance.
app_v2.playground.heading: Playground
app_v2.playground.description: Test each AI capability against its configured model.
app_v2.playground.not_configured: No model configured for this capability.
app_v2.playground.capdesc.text: Generate a text reply from a prompt.
app_v2.playground.capdesc.embeddings: Vectorise text and inspect the embedding.
app_v2.playground.capdesc.image_generati: Generate an image from a prompt.
app_v2.playground.capdesc.reranking: Reorder documents by relevance to a query.
admin.ai.defaults.ocr_engine_hint: How OpenRouter parses the PDF when the OCR model is an OpenRouter model.
admin.ai.defaults.builder_openrouter_lab: Allow OpenRouter models in the Builder
admin.ai.defaults.builder_openrouter_hin: Off by default — the Builder's tool-use loop is tuned for Claude. When on, the Builder model picker also offers enabled OpenRouter chat models. Validate a model with the builder:eval command before relying on it.
admin.ai.defaults.module.image_vision.de: Vision model for image OCR and image understanding.
app_v2.playground.capdesc.image_vision: Extract text from an image, or ask a question about it.
app_v2.playground.instructions_placehold: e.g. Mexican Spanish, warm and casual
